diagnosis: The player started at the bottom-left spawn, moved right along the floor, then ascended a tall vertical shaft via ladders (reaching y≈483 at t=5.2s), but fell back down to the mid-level corridor around y=754 and spent the remaining ~23 seconds pacing horizontally without finding a way back up or forward to the exit. The level's vertical variety and ladder density were engaging initially, but the long climb lacked mid-shaft rest points or clear wayfinding, causing a fall that reset progress without a recovery path. The player enjoyed the variance but explicitly criticized ladder design on long walls.

lessons.lesson: Long uninterrupted ladder climbs without rest platforms or horizontal traversal options cause player fatigue and falls, turning vertical traversal into a punishment loop rather than a challenge.
conditions: ladders are the sole traversal method
no horizontal ledges, platforms, or branching paths along the climb
lessons.outcome: player falls mid-climb, loses significant progress, disengages from vertical section
